title: Executive Order 10191—Extension of Trust Periods on Indian Lands Expiring During the Calendar Year 1951

flags.justification: Exercises an already-established, congressionally-anticipated trust-extension mechanism, applied uniformly, consistent with EO 8580 and EO 10027.
A uniform administrative extension is the established, standard mechanism for this trust system.
This extends a protective trust status rather than creating a compliance regime.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
A defined further term (25 years from each trust's original expiration), not an open-ended continuation.
The order explicitly does not apply where Congress has specifically reserved authority to extend trust periods to itself.
A protective trust extension, not a compliance regime requiring third-party monitoring.
A blanket, nationwide extension of trust protections with substantial consequences for Native American land tenure, consistent with EO 8580 and EO 10027.
No second agency's competing mandate is implicated.
The congressional-reservation carve-out is a protective deference to reserved congressional authority, not an undermining carve-out.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 1/14 = 7.1%. A third recurring instance of the same annual trust-extension mechanism (see EO 8580, EO 10027).
